summary.what: Tune devblog template and add operator-voice coaching to timbers prime
summary.why: The default devblog produces dry, hero-voice prose that erases the human-agent collaboration that's increasingly the actual shape of how work gets done — readers care about operator intent and the moment a teammate reshaped the call, not a sterile change-by-change accounting.
summary.how: Added 'Audience' section calling out the non-implementer reader, fourth Collaborator voice, three new tone-calibration rows, two new anti-patterns (hero voice, tour-guide voice), tightened length budget; added <operator-voice> section to prime_workflow.go with two habits (intent + collaboration) and a 'don't fabricate' guardrail; bumped devblog template version 5 → 6.

notes: Read a real custom-template devblog from osprey-strike's conduit-harness worktree to ground the diagnosis. The sample's tells: invisible agent ('I' everywhere), tour-guide pacing in the publisher-pipeline paragraph, no surfaced disagreement, resolution voice on every beat. Considered making the Collaborator voice opt-in but the bias toward solo-developer narrative is so default-on that nudging it has to be the default. Considered adding a separate 'collaboration template' but that would fragment the surface; baking it into the existing template keeps one canonical narrative shape. Length budget moved 800 → 700 because every dev blog I've read at the long end was padded.
